name: Female Student
message: "So that's... isn't it?"

name: Female Student
message: "But that's just a rumor, isn't it? All that stuff is. They're just rumors."

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, they've been saying that for ages, you know?"

name: Female Student
message: "A prediction? Something like that, right?"

name: Female Student
message: "My dad said they used to be really popular. Something about Nostradamus' prophecies."

name: Female Student
message: "Like I said, they're not prophecies."

name: Female Student
message: "If they're not prophecies, how do they tell the future?"

name: Female Student
message: "I don't know the details."

name: Female Student
message: "But they say it's scientific."

name: Female Student
message: "They call it the web bot."

name: Male Student
message: "You mean the Web Bot Project?"

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, that. You know about it?"

name: Female Student
message: "What is it?"

name: Male Student
message: "It's software that gathers all the conversations on the Internet. Apparently, it can predict the future."
Hmm. The Web Bot Project, huh?
I sat and listened to their conversation.
For some reason, that topic came up at school.
I'd only heard rumors about it.
Apparently, it was a piece of software that collected data on the Internet and, using that data as a source, analyzed the subconscious minds of large groups of people.
I didn't know who would think to make something like that, but apparently it was originally designed to predict stock prices.
Though the explanation of why they would need to make something like that to predict the stock market was a bit complicated.
It went something like this.
A new field called financial engineering was created in the 1950s by combining elements of economics, accounting, math, and traditional engineering.
The investment banks of America worked together to develop it further.
And in the mid-80's, a group of the world's greatest rocket scientists, mathematicians, and other geniuses were assembled on Wall Street.
Using complex financial derivatives, they carefully managed their risks.
These geniuses gradually pushed the beast known as the economy into a cage.
Soon, they had almost completely tamed the economy, removed its fangs, and acquired for themselves unlimited power to control it as they saw fit.
With one exception...
However...
The geniuses hid this exception away from prying eyes by calling it an "anomaly", a "contradiction", or an "exception".
The word anomaly refers to something with no clear theoretical basis, which is nevertheless clearly existent within experimental results.
It is a word to describe those patterns that defy theory.
They had a precise theory, which they used to find the patterns in their observed data.
With those tools, they completely caged the monster.
This meant that the human race had obtained the philosopher's stone... or it should have.
That's where another topic comes up.
Our speaker today will be Nassim Nicholas Taleb.
HAHAHAHAHAHA!
I have a question for you all.
The famous American index, the S&P500!
(Personally, I would start by explaining what it is, but that's just me.)
In what length of time
did the equity market
make half of its net historical gains
of the past 50 years?!
So, how long do you think it took?

name: Yuki
message: "It's hard to understand it when a foreigner says it."

name: Yuki
message: "You have to say it clearly, without worrying too much about any oversimplifications."

name: Yuki
message: "In the past 50 years of the stock market, how long did it take for it to make half of the gains it's made up till now?"
OH! That's wrong!
OH! That's wrong too!
That's totally wrong! Totally wrong!
You're not even on the right order of magnitude!
That's totally wrong! Totally wrong!
You're not even on the right order of magnitude!
If you can't even understand the scale, then the number will never be right!
NO!
NO!
NO!
NO!
NO!
THEY'RE ALL WRONG!
OH～, NO!!
DUMBASS!
NO!
NO!
NO!
NO!
NO!
IT'S ALL WRONG!
OH, NO!
DUMBASS!
Mi**ko (who?)
I'LL KILL YOU!
I'm getting ANGRY!
Huh, wha-?!
NO!
IT'S ALL WRONG!
OH, NO!
DUMBASS!
Mi**to (who?)
I'LL KILL YOU!
I'm getting ANGRY!
Eaah?!
Correct.
It was in just ten days.
In those ten days, stock prices skyrocketed.
Those ten days account for half the gains in the past fifty years.
Those irregular numbers were actually one of these exceptions.
We could ignore them, since they hardly ever happened.
Statistically, this was an exception unusual enough to be absurd.
How much of an exception?
Umm... It was about ten standard deviations away from the norm.
I guess you don't understand that.
To state it in more familiar words...
"An exception that would only happen once in the entire history of the universe since the Big Bang."
This kind of exception happens less than once every ten billion years!
So we can ignore it, right?
Huh?
Oh, you think that doesn't make sense?
And of course, you're right.
Those words, "ten standard deviations from the norm," are actually famous because a Nobel Prize winner said them after his business went bankrupt.
A hedge fund called LTCM was established by two genius economists who had won the Nobel Prize in 1997 for the Black-Scholes formula.
This hedge fund's net value grew to over seven billion dollars.
But it went bankrupt when Russia defaulted on its national debt.
At that time, the geniuses cried and said, "That was an exception that would only happen once in a hundred billion years, so we had no choice but to go bankrupt!"
How did that happen?
Truthfully, we don't know the details.
But there's one thing they should understand even if they're not geniuses (and in a way, they aren't).
In economics, there is a human element.
Which means...
Economies are run by illogical beings.
"Animal spirit."
By the way, a famous economist called Keynes coined that term.

One scholar who investigated found that the kind of massive change that is only supposed to occur every 30,000 years, according to financial engineering, has actually occurred 48 times in the 88-year history of the New York Stock Exchange.
That's totally different from the calculations.
It's true that it's completely random, but that kind of coincidence is impossible.
Though, it's not that strange to think that a human-run economy would be pulled in careless directions by the whims of the imperfect human beings.
After all, humans can't make logical decisions. They're all biased.
That's how the world works.
For instance, if everyone starts reading a boring, amateurishly written novel, it can become a bestseller, then get turned into a movie... And just like that become a fad.

Even though everyone seemed to be liberal, all of a sudden they're conservative, or wine becomes more popular than beer, or sake more popular than wine, or maybe it would be shochu... (though I'm getting off topic).
For Some Reason, Crystal!
In short, these exceptions happen because the economy is run by people who just do whatever they feel like.
Everyone overestimates or underestimates things, based on nothing more than their personal feelings.
We're a bit off topic, but... Now for the main issue.
So if you could figure out this human tendency to act "however they feel like," you could tame the economy.
If you could just understand their day-to-day whims.
How would you do that?
Let's ask a psychologist. Can't we just research the way humans make choices?
Isn't that what they call social psychology? Could you apply that to the economy?
There are economists seriously researching it (they call it behavioral economics)!
But this is a major task!
After all, they're trying to use theory to tame the anomalies that can't be explained with theory. That's gotta be hard.
But isn't there some miraculous way to get at the public subconscious?
That's where the Internet comes into play.
If you looked through people's everyday conversations scattered throughout the Internet, could you understand this tendency for people to act "however they feel like"?
It might have been impossible in the past, since you couldn't listen to everyone talking.
But now it's possible to listen to every single person's voice.
You might be able to understand the group's deepest instincts through all their individual voices.
Or maybe, by means of the outer layer we call the Internet, you could understand humanity's collective subconscious.
That belief was what lead to the creation of the Web Bot Project.
It indiscriminately collects the words scattered across the Internet and makes calculations based on figures derived from emotions, allowing it to predict things no economist could hope to predict.
The results of those calculations predict what might happen in the future. In other words, they're prophecies.
The Web Bot Project is a miraculous piece of software that made predictions from the anomaly, the subconscious, that economists had always ignored!
Amazing!
Truly amazing!
Something sounds fishy...

name: Female Student
message: "Can it really predict anything?"

name: Male Student
message: "Seems like it."
Their conversation was going in the obvious direction. I laughed a little.
Anything, huh?
Truly miraculous, huh?

name: Female Student
message: "Right, right, they say that all humans are linked subconsciously."

name: Female Student
message: "Subconsciously?"

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, a great scholar discovered it. Uh..."

name: Male Student
message: "It was Carl Gustav Jung. The collective unconscious, right?"

name: Female Student
message: "What's that?"

name: Female Student
message: "It's something shared by all humans, in the depths of our minds."

name: Female Student
message: "What's it do?"

name: Female Student
message: "It's like humanity's subconscious already knows what's going to happen in the world."

name: Female Student
message: "Oh... I see. That sounds kinda scary, though."

name: Male Student
message: "Like, just before you get sick, or get in an accident, you'll see an old lady standing at your bedside."

name: Male Student
message: "That's your subconscious trying to warn you about what's going to happen."

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, and because of that, we already subconsciously know the day humanity will go extinct."

name: Female Student
message: "I understand that, but are its predictions coming true?"

name: Male Student
message: "Yeah, it predicted the anthrax attacks in 2001, the great power outage of 2003, the earthquake in Indonesia in 2004, Hurricane Katrina in 2005, and tons of other disasters."

name: Female Student
message: "I-I see. That's kind of creepy."

name: Female Student
message: "And the Web Robot is predicting that something big is going to happen this year."

name: Male Student
message: "It's not a robot. It's called a bot."

name: Female Student
message: "Something big?"

name: Male Student
message: "It says humanity is going to reach the peak of this string of recent disasters."

name: Female Student
message: "The peak of disaster?"

name: Female Student
message: "The end of the world."

name: Female Student
message: "Th-The end of the world?"

name: Female Student
message: "That's what it's predicting."

name: Female Student
message: "The end of the world... No way."

name: Female Student
message: "It says the world is full of anxious words."
My smile disappeared when I heard her say that.

name: Yuki
message: "Full of... anxious words."

name: Female Student
message: "Full of anxious words?"

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, the world on the Internet is filled with tons of anxious words."

name: Zakuro
message: "The sky is full of anxious words."

name: Female Student
message: "And that anxiety is seeping into tons of other words."

name: Female Student
message: "It's like the Internet itself is absorbing the anxiety..."

name: Female Student
message: "Wh-What the heck? I don't get it. That's too scary."

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, but that's what it says."

name: Female Student
message: "The Web Bot Project's data shows that it's just like the anxiety in the heart of a person condemned to death."

name: Female Student
message: "Just like a person condemned to death..."

name: Man
message: "And... the Web Bot..."
A heart seeping with anxiety...
Just like a person condemned to death...
That's...

name: Zakuro
message: "The sky is full of anxious words."

name: Zakuro
message: "The process of absorption has begun."

name: Zakuro
message: "The world..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Anxious words, and the world that has absorbed them."

name: Zakuro
message: "Do you know what those are?"

name: Zakuro
message: "The heart of the person who will soon die."

name: Zakuro
message: "Therefore, the world..."

name: Female Student
message: "Is the world going to end?"

name: Female Student
message: "Supposedly."
Yesterday, they were all talking about Takashima-san's suicide, but now they were talking about the end of the world.
How did they end up at this topic?
It kind of made me want to laugh.
But to be honest, I didn't feel like I could even force out a laugh at the moment.

name: Zakuro
message: "Therefore, the world..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Will end."
I'd tried my best to not think about that, since it was such a ridiculous thing to say.
But even though it was a ridiculous thing to say, it was still ringing in my ears.
The end of the world...
I thought that meant...
The end of 'her world'.
In other words, I thought those words referred to her death.
That's what I had thought she meant.
Or rather, that was what I wanted to think she meant.
But somehow, something deep inside me, something I'd forgotten, was telling me that those words were dangerous.
You could call that part my subconscious.
It was like my subconscious knew what Takashima-san meant by those words.
Something deep down inside me was chattering.
Someone in my inner heart, someone who wasn't unrelated to me, was whispering something...
Surely that was just an illusion...

name: Yuki
message: "No, no way. This is just a coincidence. As if something like that could happen. Ahaha."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? What?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "You know, Yuki."

name: Kagami
message: "Wait, Tsukasa, you―"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Owgh."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What? What's wrong?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "You know, Yuki."

name: Kagami
message: "Tsukasa, what are you―"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Owwaah..."

name: Yuki
message: "What? What is it?"

name: Kagami
message: "N-None of your business. D-Don't worry about us."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Y-Yuki..."
Tsukasa was lying on the floor, saying something.

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yuki... Delivery."

name: Yuki
message: "Delivery?"

name: Kagami
message: "Huh?"
Tsukasa handed me something wrapped in cloth.

name: Kagami
message: "Wait, that's―"

name: Kagami
message: "Uwah! Tsukasa!"
Tsukasa grabbed Kagami's leg to stop her from moving.

name: Kagami
message: "Ts-Tsukasa. Let go of me!"

name: Tsukasa
message: "I-I'm not letting go!"

name: Kagami
message: "Tsukasa!"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ooohh..."
Kagami was always so kind to Tsukasa, but now she was kicking her on the floor.
And Tsukasa always listened to what Kagami said, but now she was on the floor, ignoring Kagami's words.
Amazing. What a rare sight.

name: Yuki
message: "U-Um, Tsukasa?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "D-Don't worry about me. Hurry up and open it."

name: Yuki
message: "No, it's not that I'm worried about you, but..."

name: Kagami
message: "D-Don't!"
I was a bit troubled, but I opened the package.
And...

name: Yuki
message: "A packed lunch?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Onee-chan made it!"

name: Yuki
message: "Okay..."

name: Tsukasa
message: "It's yours!"

name: Yuki
message: "No, it's not mine."

name: Tsukasa
message: "No! Onee-chan made it for you."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Kagami
message: "Th-That's not it..."

name: Kagami
message: "That's not it... That's not what it is... Um, well..."

name: Yuki
message: "What?"

name: Kagami
message: "Yuki, do you like G**dam?"

name: Yuki
message: "G**dam? Why?"

name: Kagami
message: "B-Because, if you stick... If you stick this box in your G**dam, it'll be three times as fast as it is now."

name: Yuki
message: "This lunchbox will make it three times as fast?! So you're saying it's a Tem Ray lunchbox?"

name: Kagami
message: "Wh-Who's that?"

name: Yuki
message: "Don't bring up the show like that if you haven't watched it."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Onee-chan!"
I wondered what she was freaking out about. I didn't get what she was saying at all.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, Kagami..."

name: Kagami
message: "Yuki, about lunch..."

name: Yuki
message: "I haven't bought any yet."

name: Kagami
message: "I see. I heard there's a nice pastaria by the station."

name: Yuki
message: "Right."

name: Kagami
message: "I heard that they serve lunch around this time."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, it would be pretty amazing if they were serving dinner at that time..."

name: Kagami
message: "Yeah, so why don't you go there?"

name: Yuki
message: "Why would I?"

name: Kagami
message: "B-Because I don't want an eyesore like you around while I'm eating, of course!"

name: Yuki
message: "Th-That's ridiculous."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Jeez! Onee-chan!"

name: Kagami
message: "I-It's so hot. You can't expect us to eat here in the middle of summer."

name: Yuki
message: "It's fine. Don't worry. Just be quiet and follow me."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Wow, it feels nice here. I didn't know about this spot."

name: Kagami
message: "Well, Yuki is a professional slacker after all. I didn't even know this place existed."

name: Yuki
message: "There's no direct sunlight here, and a cool wind blows in from the forest. It's a nice place."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yeah, good job, Yuki. I guess skipping class isn't a complete waste of time after all."

name: Yuki
message: "Now Tsukasa is criticizing me too."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Oh, no, that was a compliment. You're good at skipping class. It's a compliment."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't get it."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Anyway, let's eat."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, let's."

name: Yuki
message: "From the outside, it appears to be a normal lunchbox."

name: Kagami
message: "What are you trying to say?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Not just from the outside, though. The inside is pretty normal too."

name: Yuki
message: "When you say normal, it sounds like you're saying it's plain. Like it's not very good."

name: Kagami
message: W-Well, of course it's..."

name: Kagami
message: "Stare..."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Staaare..."

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you two sitting there and waiting for me to eat?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahaha, we're not waiting or anything."

name: Yuki
message: "Then what is it?"

name: Kagami
message: "Sh-Shut up! Just eat it already, idiot!"

name: Yuki
message: "Why does that make me an idiot?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Um, Yuki, Onee-chan was probably waiting to see if you liked it."

name: Kagami
message: "N-Not at all. I just... I was gonna hit you if you said it wasn't good."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So I should be on my guard after taking a bite?"

name: Kagami
message: "So you're assuming it's not going to be good?"

name: Kagami
message: "Hey! Why are you on your guard?"

name: Yuki
message: "Aha☆!"

name: Kagami
message: "I-If it's not good, just say so. Why won't you say anything?"

name: Yuki
message: "Ah, this is a joke. I'm joking."

name: Yuki
message: "It's just..."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Just what?"

name: Yuki
message: "What is this?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Aha, that's octopus sausage."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, really? Then what is this?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "That's crab sausage."

name: Yuki
message: "Then what is this?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Clam sausage."

name: Yuki
message: "Why clam?"

name: Kagami
message: "Sh-Shut up. Just eat it already. It's going to get cold at this rate."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't think a lunchbox could get any colder."

name: Kagami
message: "Sh-Shut up already! Who cares about that?!"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Look, the beetle sausage is really cute."

name: Yuki
message: "Beetle sausage?!"

name: Kagami
message: "Yes. Got a problem with it?"

name: Yuki
message: "I don't have a problem, but anyone would be surprised by that."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Onee-chan tried to make a lot of different things, but they didn't come out too well."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, I see. So she just gave them all names based on what they ended up looking like."

name: Kagami
message: "S-So how is it?"

name: Yuki
message: "How's what?"

name: Kagami
message: "Th-The taste! If it's bad, just say so."

name: Yuki
message: "It tastes good. It's really delicious."

name: Kagami
message: "R-Really?"

name: Yuki
message: "But they don't look so nice."

name: Kagami
message: "S-Sorry... about that."

name: Yuki
message: "Ahahaha, don't worry about it. It tastes great."

name: Tsukasa
message: "So what do you do up here when you're skipping class?"

name: Yuki
message: "Read books, take naps, modify my G**dam to move three times as fast, stuff like that."

name: Kagami
message: "What's that? Are you making fun of me?"

name: Yuki
message: "Aha☆!"

name: Kagami
message: "You are."

name: Yuki
message: "Right you are, Kawasaki-san."

name: Kagami
message: "You've always liked being alone, haven't you?"
She ignored my joke like she hadn't even heard it.
No worries, though. She was always like this.

name: Yuki
message: "You think so? It's not really that I like being alone, it's just that I hate being around annoying people."

name: Kagami
message: "Isn't that the same thing?"

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe it is."

name: Tsukasa
message: "But you're always off by yourself, so there's tons of stuff we don't know about you."

name: Yuki
message: "You mean, like, 'We always try to get closer to you, Yuki, but you always push us away!' or something like that?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Not really. Lately, you've been like a complete stranger. You hardly ever talk to us anymore."

name: Kagami
message: "Yeah."

name: Kagami
message: "We actually had no idea what you were doing up here."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Even though we used to hang out all the time..."

name: Kagami
message: "Wh-What's wrong?"

name: Yuki
message: "N-Nothing. It's nothing."
What was that?
My vision kind of blurred.

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yuki, are you feeling all right?"

name: Yuki
message: "I'm fine. It's probably because I was up so late last night."

name: Kagami
message: "What were you doing up so late?"

name: Yuki
message: "Well, you see..."

name: Yuki
message: "I was thinking about you, Kagami."

name: Kagami
message: "Huh?"

name: Kagami
message: "Wh-What do you mean? Why would you be thinking about me?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, nothing. Just that stupid face you made."

name: Kagami
message: "When did I make a stupid face?!"

name: Yuki
message: "Well, your face looks stupid more often than not, so..."

name: Yuki
message: "You don't have to hit me that hard!"

name: Kagami
message: "I'll hit you as hard as I want!"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Oooh."

name: Yuki
message: "What, Tsukasa?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "I wish you would think about me, even if it was about a stupid face I made."

name: Yuki
message: "You don't make stupid faces very often, though."

name: Tsukasa
message: "I'll... I'll go practice making stupid faces, then."

name: Kagami
message: "Please don't practice something like that."

name: Yuki
message: "I was thinking about Tsukasa."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "Her dumb face."

name: Kagami
message: "Are you stupid?!"

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you the one hitting me?!"

name: Kagami
message: "Of course I'm going to hit you for that."
Tsukasa stared at me.

name: Tsukasa
message: "Heheh."

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you laughing all of a sudden?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahaha, sorry. Just thinking that you're still the same Yuki we knew in the past."

name: Yuki
message: "In the past?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "I would always sit in class and wonder what kind of face you were making at the moment."

name: Yuki
message: "What kind of face? The same as always?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Maybe so, but it's the same, and yet different. It's a face I don't know."

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "I'm not really sure, but that's how it feels."

name: Tsukasa
message: "After you showed us this place, I got this feeling like you were still wearing yesterday's face."

name: Yuki
message: "Yesterday?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahahaha, it's nothing."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Now that you mention it..."

name: Tsukasa
message: "It seems like some strange rumors are going around in class."
I guess she heard them too, since we're in the same class and all.

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. I don't know what's up with them, but they've been talking about a lot of funny stuff."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, rumors like that only stay around for a little while. They're like measles: everyone will get over them soon enough."

name: Kagami
message: "They're not."

name: Yuki
message: "Not? Not what?"

name: Kagami
message: "All that stuff they're talking about. They say it's proof."

name: Yuki
message: "Proof? What do you mean?"

name: Kagami
message: "It's really stupid, but they say Takashima-san committed suicide in preparation for the end of the world."
I could feel the muscles in my face tensing up.
I almost expected it to come up eventually.
I answered in the calmest voice I could manage.

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm. Preparation, huh? How was that preparation?"

name: Kagami
message: "I heard the world was going to end on the 20th, and Takashima-san had to die for it to happen."

name: Yuki
message: "She had to die? Why?"

name: Kagami
message: "I don't know that."

name: Yuki
message: "But still, an apocalypse on the 20th, huh?"

name: Kagami
message: "Yeah."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. Pretty soon, isn't it? So who said that? Did Takashima-san say that while she was still alive?"

name: Kagami
message: "I don't really know, but I haven't heard anyone say Takashima-san said that."
I see.
I'd heard her say a lot of enigmatic things, but she never said the world was going to end on the 20th.
Where did that date come from?

name: Tsukasa
message: "There are rumors Shiroyama-kun's incident is related too. And I'm not sure, but I don't think Takashima-san was the one that started the rumors."

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Apparently, there are a lot of people talking about it on the Internet."

name: Yuki
message: "On the Internet?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yeah. Miu said there's a thread about our school on one of the anonymous message boards."

name: Yuki
message: "Anonymous message board? You mean the one that got sold off to some foreigner in Singapore?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "From what Miu saw, the thread was full of those rumors."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So when did that stuff about the end of the world and Takashima-san's suicide start coming up?"

name: Kagami
message: "Miu said she couldn't really tell."

name: Yuki
message: "But someone had to say it first, right? Someone must've come in and started talking about how the world was going to end."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Maybe, but it seems like all the fuss was caused by a spark from somewhere else."

name: Yuki
message: "A spark? From where?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "I'm not sure. They're talking about it on our school's site, a lot of occult sites, and a bunch of other places, so it's hard to tell where it first started."

name: Kagami
message: "The origin... would probably be the underground message board, wouldn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "What's that?"

name: Kagami
message: "There was a bunch of controversy about those before."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, they were talking about those on the news a while back."
An underground message board. Even our school has one of those.
Though I suppose most of the schools in the country have them, so that's to be expected.
The "underground" part of the name would make you think people are doing illegal stuff there, but it just means that it's an unofficial school site.
At its core, it's a message board where people from the same school can talk online.
But even a mere message board could cause problems.
Apparently, they can become hotbeds for slander, bullying, and crime.
That's why the news did a bunch of stories on them.

name: Yuki
message: "I see. The underground message board, huh?"
If a shady site like that existed, then that was probably where they heard about the Web Bot Project.
I was surprised to hear a phrase like that in class, but now it made sense.
But still...

name: Yuki
message: "But still, the 20th is too soon. It's just a week away."

name: Kagami
message: "Apparently, there have been rumors about it for a while."

name: Yuki
message: "For a while?"

name: Kagami
message: "Yeah, it looks like the theory about the world ending on July 20th has been around for quite a while."

name: Yuki
message: "How long is quite a while?"

name: Kagami
message: "I don't know..."

name: Yuki
message: "Was it Takashima-san who said it, after all?"

name: Kagami
message: "I don't know that, either. I haven't looked at the message board myself."
So neither Tsukasa nor Kagami had seen it themselves.

name: Kagami
message: "U-Um, do you think it's a prophecy?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahahaha. Onee-chan and I were talking about it last night. It's kind of scary. If it's a prophecy, do you think it'll actually come true?"

name: Yuki
message: "Who knows?"

name: Kagami
message: "Y-Yeah..."

name: Yuki
message: "But I think you would call that a delusion, not a prophecy."

name: Tsukasa
message: "A delusion?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah. As far as I know, every person that has ever prophesied the end of the world before has turned out to be delusional."

name: Kagami
message: "Y-You mean..."

name: Yuki
message: "They've never come true, so this one won't come true either."

name: Kagami
message: "Y-Yeah, you're right. That's definitely it."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahahaha. I told her you'd say that."
A prophecy, huh?
Whether you call it a prophecy or one of the Web Bot Project's predictions, it's still just a delusion and nothing more.
There's no way you could ever perfectly predict what was going to happen in such a complicated world.
Even a grade schooler would understand that.
Or they should understand that, anyway.
But still, people forget that obvious truth.
The obvious becomes less than obvious.
For instance...
For instance, if everyone in the world believed we were about to go extinct.
Of course, that's impossible, but if everyone believed it, it might actually happen.
If the people believe it...
The infinitesimally small chance... The kind of calamity that should only happen every ten billion years...
That can actually happen every ten or twenty years, abetted by the illogical human heart.
Animal spirit.
A swarm of illogical souls.
Their overconfidence and their exaggerated fears occasion the impossible.
If the people believe it possible, it becomes possible.
But still, that won't happen. There's a big difference between the stock market crashing and the end of the world happening.
Besides, I doubt everyone in the world would ever really believe such a thing.
Tons of people used to say Nostradamus was a prophet, but none of his predictions ever came true.
Not the end of the world. Not even a natural disaster.
The people didn't truly believe.
So nothing happened.
But what would happen if a situation was created where everyone truly believed?
If a group of people was locked away in a closed space and they were all made to believe the same thing...
And if it wasn't a prophecy made by someone in a far off country, but rather a revelation brought about by the death of one of their own...
It's not all that uncommon for fatalistic cults to commit group suicides. There have been plenty of incidents like that in the past.
The world will end on the 20th?
Had Takashima-san said anything like that?
All I heard her say was that the world was going to end.
Or maybe Takashima-san never said when it would happen. Would that mean the date just came from some embellished rumor?
If that was true, there didn't have to be any real basis for the date.
Either way...
It was spooky.

name: Kagami
message: "Yuki."

name: Kagami
message: "Hey, Yuki! Lunchtime is over."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Oh... Sorry."
I wanted to investigate a bit.
Just what was going on?
I wanted to know.

name: Female Student
message: "I saw it earlier, but now everyone's talking about it."

name: Female Student
message: "They were linking to it on other sites."

name: Female Student
message: "Yeah, the posts were all about the Web Bot Project."

name: Male Student
message: "It's just like I said."

name: Female Student
message: "I wonder if it's real."

name: Male Student
message: "What?"

name: Female Student
message: "The Web Bot Project says the world will be destroyed on the 20th."

name: Male Student
message: "You saw the link, didn't you? It said that's what the data was showing."

name: Female Student
message: "I know, but that's scary."

name: Yuki
message: "You know..."

name: Male Student
message: "Huh?"

name: Male Student
message: "Ah, Minakami-san."

name: Yuki
message: "And you're dumbass number one."

name: Male Student
message: "Um, that's not my name. I'm Kiyoshi. Yokoyama Kiyoshi."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, that's your name?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Um, Minakami-san, would you please remember it this time? We are in the same class, after all."
The boy in front of me was Yokoyama Kiyoshi.
Eh, who cares about him?
Average looks. Average grades. Not anyone I cared about.
Though I wasn't really interested in boys anyway.

name: Kiyoshi
message: "It's awfully rare for you to talk to me."

name: Yuki
message: "I actually don't want to talk to you at all, but I have something I need to ask you, so whatever."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Something you want to ask me? Wow, I'm honored."
Did this guy like me or something? Gross.

name: Yuki
message: "That Web Bot Project you were talking about. Is there really a site like that, where they predict the future?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Of course there is!"

name: Yuki
message: "That's one of those Web Bots that automatically goes around the Internet and collects various information to store in a searchable database, right?"

name: "Then it's simply a bot that generates HTTP requests, retrieves some HTML documents, searches for keywords within it, and saves them in the database. Then it can analyze the document's anchor tags and use the first file's links to retrieve additional HTML files."
message: Yuki

name: Yuki
message: "Then it's simply a bot that generates HTTP requests, retrieves some HTML documents, searches for keywords within it, and saves them in the database. Then it can analyze the document's anchor tags and use the first file's links to retrieve additional HTML files."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "I don't get it..."

name: Yuki
message: "To put it simply, the Web Bot is a search engine with spiders and web crawlers."

name: Yuki
message: "What's this Web Bot Project that's supposedly making those predictions, though? What's the website?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Um, I don't really know myself. It's just a topic on the message board."

name: Yuki
message: "The underground message board..."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "You know about it, then?"

name: Yuki
message: "No, not really, but I've heard rumors. Actually, have you ever been to the site?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Yeah, of course I have."

name: Yuki
message: "Then could you give me the URL?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Sure, but you have to register to get past the front page, and you can't even register without a referral."

name: Yuki
message: "Registration? I thought it was anonymous?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Yeah, but what would be the point if it was open to the public?"

name: Yuki
message: "So that's how it works."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Yeah."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, that's fine. Anyway, send me the URL, and then give me a referral."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "What's your phone's email address? I can send it right now."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't have one."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Huh? You don't have a cell phone?"

name: Yuki
message: "It ticks me off that you think everyone has one. Don't go around assuming everyone has a cell phone."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "R-Really? Um... you don't, huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "What's your problem? I don't have one. It doesn't exist."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "But still, everyone browses that site with their cell phones. I don't think I've ever seen anyone go there on a computer."

name: Yuki
message: "Just send it to my computer. I always get on the Internet from a desktop computer."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Sure... Tell me your email address, then."

name: Yuki
message: "Here."
I wrote down my email address and passed it to him.

name: Kiyoshi
message: "This is a free mail account."

name: Yuki
message: "That's right. Is there a problem with that?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "No, but don't you have a real email address?"

name: Yuki
message: "I do, but I'm not giving it to you."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Um... I have a favor to ask."

name: Yuki
message: "What?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "If I become your referrer, that means we're friends, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "You shouldn't say such nasty things with a grin on your face. So then, what are you trying to say?"

name: Kiyoshi
message: "There's this party pretty soon... If you'd like to come, I could get you in."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Oogh."

name: Yuki
message: "You remember what happened the last time you guys tried hitting on me, don't you?"

name: Yuki
message: "I hate men. If you start talking like that again, I'll do to you what I did to them, but otherwise I don't mind."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Um, we actually might need you to do something like that. We need someone strong."
Huh?
Why did they need someone strong for a party?
This guy was weird.

name: Yuki
message: "I don't really know what you mean, but I don't mind participating."

name: Kiyoshi
message: "Hell yeah. We haven't had a main attraction at all since Shiroyama-kun died."
That was more confusing.
Why was I replacing Shiroyama-kun?
He was that idiot who died in an accident, right? What's he got to do with it?
Anyway...
Looks like it really was the underground message board.
The girls earlier said there were a bunch of links posted, so it shouldn't be hard to investigate.

name: Yuki
message: "I hope I can figure out what's going on."
Aside from that...
Most message boards like that were full of people slandering their teachers and classmates.
Which meant they were probably talking about me too.
For some reason...
That was a bit embarrassing.

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yuki."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Oh."

name: Kagami
message: "Haa... What are you making that stupid face for? Class is over."

name: Yuki
message: "Is it? I was asleep."

name: Kagami
message: "You were awake. Don't make excuses like that."

name: Kagami
message: "So were you thinking about something?"

name: Yuki
message: "You were the one that was thinking about something."

name: Kagami
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "You were both thinking about me, right? She's so beautiful, I wish I was like her. Stuff like that."

name: Kagami
message: "Tsukasa, let's go."

name: Yuki
message: "You ignored me."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yuki, we won't mind if you go home without us."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm... I have something to do, so I can't go home just yet."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Huh?"

name: Kagami
message: "You don't have to do that for us. We'll be fine."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yeah. I don't want to keep you waiting."

name: Yuki
message: "I-It's not like I'm doing it for you or anything."

name: Kagami
message: "What was that?"

name: Yuki
message: "My best Kagami impression."

name: Kagami
message: "D-Don't make fun of me!"

name: Yuki
message: "My cheek hurts..."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Ahahaha."

name: Yuki
message: "But I really do have stuff I need to do. I'll probably be here until you guys finish up, so call me when you're done."

name: Kagami
message: "How are we going to call you when you don't even have a cell phone?"

name: Yuki
message: "Dunno."

name: Tsukasa
message: "What do you mean, 'dunno'?"
Something was bothering me...
There were a lot of things that were still bothering me.
Takashima Zakuro...
I wanted to know a little more about her.

name: Yuki
message: "Takashima-san's class was right over here..."

name: Yasuko
message: "Oh, Minakami-senpai."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, you're..."

name: Yasuko
message: "Jeez, how many times do I have to tell you before you'll remember?"

name: Yuki
message: "Got it. I'll remember it this time."

name: Yasuko
message: "You said that last time too."

name: Yuki
message: "Did I?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Yokoyama Yasuko. Kiyoshi's little sister."

name: Yuki
message: "Um, who's Kiyoshi?"

name: Yasuko
message: "How could you not remember my brother? He's in your class!"

name: Yuki
message: "Like I said, I'll remember it this time."

name: Yasuko
message: "No, not remembering someone in the same class is a little strange."

name: Yasuko
message: "Come on, he's the one you punched when he was hitting on you."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, that guy's gross!"

name: Yasuko
message: "Um, please don't call my brother gross. For the record, we are related, so it kind of feels like you're talking about me too."

name: Yuki
message: "So then, what's up, Yokoyama Kiyoshi's little sister?"

name: Yasuko
message: "What? Say my name!"

name: Yuki
message: "Um, Yokoyama..."

name: Yuki
message: "Kn*ck?"

name: Yasuko
message: "No! That's not even a girl's name!"

name: Yuki
message: "Yokoyama Knocketta!"

name: Yasuko
message: "No! That's not even a real name! It's Yokoyama Yasuko, and you'd better remember it this time!"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, right. That's what it was."

name: Yasuko
message: "I hope you're ready for the consequences if you don't remember it next time."

name: Yuki
message: "Like I said, I'll remember it this time."

name: Yasuko
message: "Really?"

name: Yuki
message: "Trust me."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm?"

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you still here? Kagami went to her club."

name: Yuki
message: "What's wrong, Yokoyama Sisterko?"

name: Yasuko
message: "You're going to make me mad. I'll hurt you. My name is Yasuko!"

name: Yuki
message: "Ah, Yasuko. I knew that."

name: Yasuko
message: "No, you didn't."

name: Yuki
message: "Of course I did! You should believe the words of your elders!"

name: Yasuko
message: "My brother said you're pretty smart."

name: Yuki
message: "Of course. My mind has even grasped the secrets of the universe."

name: Yasuko
message: "What are my three sizes?"

name: Yuki
message: "From the top, bust 100 centimeters, waist 100, hip 101."

name: Yasuko
message: "What am I, a cube?!"

name: Yuki
message: "It's a joke. They're actually 102 for your bust, 20 for your waist, and 102 for your hips."

name: Yasuko
message: "No, my body isn't that great..."

name: Yuki
message: "Is that all you wanted to know?"

name: Yasuko
message: "That was a joke."

name: Yuki
message: "Whaaat?!"

name: Yasuko
message: "I don't understand why you would be mad about that."

name: Yuki
message: "I tried to make a joke of my own."

name: Yasuko
message: "This conversation isn't going anywhere."

name: Yuki
message: "All right. So what did you actually want to ask me?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Will the world end this month, on the 20th?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Is it true that the end of the world is approaching?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Please, answer me!"

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you asking me?"

name: Yasuko
message: "I heard you were the only one that got along with Takashima Zakuro-san. So I thought you would know the truth."
It wasn't like I got along with Takashima-san that well.
Jeez, everyone just assumed that.
Haa...

name: Yuki
message: "Well, let me be serious."

name: Yuki
message: "Long ago, people gathered to get rid of all the conflict in the world. They tried to bring all the weapons in the world to one place."

name: Yuki
message: "Then all those weapons coalesced into a single organism."

name: Yuki
message: "This organism was incredibly violent. It had the form of an All-Purpose Humanoid Battle Weapon."

name: Yasuko
message: "What's that?"

name: Yuki
message: "It was the Pacifist Robo-Gandhi."

name: Yasuko
message: "What are you talking about?!"

name: Yasuko
message: "You just said it was incredibly violent, so how is it a Pacifist Robot?!"

name: Yuki
message: "It employs pacifism against its enemies."

name: Yuki
message: "Pacifist Mode! Pacifism! Pacifism! Pacifism! All Systems Pacifist!"

name: Yuki
message: "Pacifist Robot, launch!"

name: Yuki
message: "Like that. He attacks with pacifism."

name: Yasuko
message: "He would lose, though. He couldn't beat anyone with pacifism!"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, he always gets clobbered."

name: Yuki
message: "Actually, there's something I'd like to ask you. Why would the world end so suddenly?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Huh? How did we get back to the serious conversation?"

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean? I was serious the whole time."

name: Yasuko
message: "Y-You were just making up a bunch of stuff, weren't you?"

name: Yuki
message: "The words of the wise may appear irrelevant at first, but after careful consideration you'll find they were linked to the true message."

name: Yuki
message: "I'm always serious. I don't mess around."

name: Yasuko
message: "I-I see... So you're saying that the story about Robo-Gandhi was foreshadowing for something else?"

name: Yuki
message: "Of course it was! I'm not one of those scenario writers that just keeps writing and writing and can't wrap up all his plot points!"

name: Yasuko
message: "Wh-Who are you talking about?"

name: Yuki
message: "It's a secret! But back to what you were talking about before..."

name: Yasuko
message: "Yeah, everyone is saying that the world will be destroyed on the 20th."

name: Yuki
message: "So you believe them?"

name: Yasuko
message: "But it's strange, isn't it? Two people have died in such a short time."

name: Yuki
message: "It definitely is strange that two people from the same school fell to their deaths one after the other, but why exactly does that mean the world's going to end?"

name: Yasuko
message: "They say that's the reason why Takashima-senpai committed suicide."

name: Yuki
message: "How do they know that's the reason she committed suicide?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Well, tons of people are talking about it on the message board, and that email is going around."
So she'd been looking at the underground message board too.
But what email was she talking about?

name: Yuki
message: "What email are you talking about? What's going around?"

name: Yasuko
message: "This."

name: Yuki
message: "This..."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?!"
through death i have been reborn as a warrior
or so they said but
it hurts
i have no body but it still hurts
it hurts because i've been broken
therefore
everyone must die
you will die in 6 days
all of you will die

name: Yuki
message: "What... is this?"
The picture was a bit fuzzy, but it was definitely a picture of her suicide.
Who would take such a distasteful picture?

name: Yasuko
message: "This is... Takashima-senpai's email address."

name: Yuki
message: "W-Wait a second. Were you friends with her?"

name: Yasuko
message: "I wasn't. I haven't even seen her before. Why would I be getting an email from her?"

name: Yuki
message: "Then how do you know it's her mail address?"
If you thought about it, it had to be a prank. But I understood why she was upset.
Anyone would be upset if they were sent a freaky picture like this.

name: Yuki
message: "Were you the only one who got this email?"

name: Yasuko
message: "A lot of people in my class did."

name: Yuki
message: "Not all of them?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Not all of them, but my brother said he got one too."
Him too, huh?
But he was still talking about going to a party. Maybe thoughtless guys aren't as sensitive to horror?

name: Yasuko
message: "Senpai! What's happening here?"

name: Yuki
message: "Calm down. Just try to stay calm, okay?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Calm?"

name: Yasuko
message: "There's no way I can stay calm. I'm scared."

name: Yuki
message: "Okay, here's a question. Why would Takashima-san send you a picture of her corpse?"

name: Yuki
message: "And look at the camera angle. It must have been taken by a third party."

name: Yasuko
message: "Th-That's because the scene witnessed by Takashima-san's ghost became a picture and got sent out in the email."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. You're pretty smart, aren't they?"

name: Yasuko
message: "That's just what people are saying on the message board."

name: Yuki
message: "I see. So then why does the person who took the picture have a shadow?"

name: Yasuko
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "The shadow was pretty long, since it was late in the evening. If you look closely, there's clearly a shadow stretching from the position of the camera."

name: Yasuko
message: "Th-That's... her ghost's shadow."

name: Yuki
message: "Do ghosts have shadows? Either way, the shadow here doesn't have long hair. Takashima-san's hair was long."

name: Yuki
message: "Of course, the outline is blurred, but this looks like the shadow of someone with short hair."

name: Yuki
message: "This has to be someone who was passing by when she committed suicide. They probably took the picture out of morbid interest, and then they used it for this prank."

name: Yasuko
message: "B-But her name is in the email address."

name: Yuki
message: "It would be easy to fool people by registering an email address with her name in it. Want me to show you how easy it is?"

name: Yuki
message: "Now, now. Don't worry about it too much."

name: Yasuko
message: "Wh-Why not?"

name: Yuki
message: "I'll investigate it."

name: Yasuko
message: "Huh?"

name: Yuki
message: "Even before I promised you, I planned on investigating this anyway."

name: Yasuko
message: "R-Really?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah."

name: Yasuko
message: "You'll really check it out?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, leave it to me."

name: Yasuko
message: "Do you know something about what's going on?"

name: Yuki
message: "That's what I'm trying to figure out, isn't it?"

name: Yuki
message: "For now, just forward that email to my computer."

name: Yasuko
message: "Huh? Not to your phone?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, I don't have a phone."

name: Yasuko
message: "That's awfully rare nowadays..."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, maybe, but there are still people who don't."

name: Yuki
message: "Anyway, there's no way the world's going to end in the near future. That's ridiculous."

name: Yasuko
message: "Okay, got it. I'm sorry if I bothered you."

name: Yasuko
message: "All right, time for my club!"

name: Yuki
message: "Do your best."
Yokoyama's little sister, huh? She was in the kendo club, right?
Which meant she was in the same club as Tsukasa and Kagami?

name: Yasuko
message: "Excuse me."

name: Yuki
message: "Yes?"

name: Yasuko
message: "So what was the foreshadowing for?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Yasuko
message: "The story about Robo-Gandhi. You said that was foreshadowing for something or other."

name: Yasuko
message: "But even at the end, it still sounded like something you made up on the spot."

name: Yuki
message: "The meaning was..."

name: Yuki
message: "You can't save someone with violence alone."

name: Yuki
message: "It was...
a criticism of our modern society, and..."

name: Yasuko
message: "Hey! That wasn't related to the conversation at all, was it?"

name: Yuki
message: "Not really, no."
Ugh...
Honestly, that picture sent chills up my spine.
What the hell was that about? Emailing people pictures of a suicide was beyond juvenile.
It scared me a bit too.
But I wondered if I'd really managed to convince Knocketta back there.
Nothing good had happened lately.
Takashima Zakuro's classroom...
Her desk was decorated with a vase of flowers.
No one was in the classroom.
An email from Takashima-san, huh?
I don't know who thought up that prank, but that person definitely isn't an exemplary citizen in our modern society.

name: Yuki
message: "A prank..."

name: Yuki
message: "Though this is quite the elaborate prank."
I wondered if they had some other motive for the prank.
Oh?
I sound like a real detective now, don't I? Cool.

name: Yuki
message: "Haa... I don't really want to go through a dead person's belongings, but..."
I did promise Yokoyama Sisterko, too.
There was nothing in Takashima-san's desk.
I suppose that should've been obvious.
If there was anything there before, it was probably taken as articles of the deceased.

name: Yuki
message: "Of course..."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm?"

name: Yuki
message: "Whoa?"
While I was searching around, I felt something slimy in the back of Takashima-san's desk.

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What? Is something rotting back there?"

name: Yuki
message: "What's this?"
It wasn't a solid, but a liquid. It was like the slime a snail leaves behind it.

name: Yuki
message: "I'm going to pretend I didn't see that. But first, I'd better wipe it up."

name: Yuki
message: "Putting that aside..."
I carefully looked at the top of her desk.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this?"

name: Yuki
message: "Spiral... Matai?"

name: Yuki
message: "What the heck is this?"
She had apparently carved those words into her desk.

name: Yuki
message: "The only way to describe this is 'creepy'."
I was under the impression that people thought she was weird because of her personality, but looking at this desk, I could see why.
Or did the bullying make her like this?
They were just scribbles, but...
It was spooky to think that this was what she was writing just before her death.
I took out the detective kit I'd brought with me. It contained a magnifying glass, a fingerprinting kit, a blood testing kit, and more.
It cost 18,000 yen, but it was really high quality.
I calmly took the magnifying glass out and examined each of the letters.

name: Yuki
message: "This life is worth three billion gildans."

name: Yuki
message: "The third wave..."

name: Yuki
message: "Uguu..."
This is...
This is...!
When I looked more closely, I realized that those were the scribbles on my magnifying glass.
Ahahaha...
I'm such a trickster.

name: Yuki
message: "Back to work."

name: Yuki
message: "Brain... Reverse..."

name: Yuki
message: "Har Megiddo..."

name: Yuki
message: "Nebula Cloud... Elhimuro."
It was all the same.
Totally incomprehensible.

name: Yuki
message: "Angel Remover... Angel Night... I am Angel Advise..."

name: Yuki
message: "What is this? I don't get it."
I jotted it all down.
It would be so convenient if I had a camera right now.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, what else?"

name: Yuki
message: "Hm? This..."
I couldn't see it very well because it was blurry. Or rather, she scratched it out after writing it.
And quite a few times, too. Why would she do that?
In one spot, it was like she'd written something several times and crossed it out every time she wrote it.
I took out the aluminum powder.
This was a very important part of the detective kit.

name: Yuki
message: "Heheh... With this, your secrets will be revealed!"
I start dusting the desk and taking prints.
This detective kit was amazing.
I pulled up a bunch of fingerprints, making the letters hard to read.

name: Yuki
message: "Hey! I'm not getting anything but fingerprints."
Well, I guess it was a fingerprinting kit.

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, there are so many fingerprints, I can't read the letters."
I kept the fingerprints anyway.

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe they'll come in handy later."

name: Yuki
message: "It looks like there are two different sets of fingerprints here."
Only two... It made you think about how isolated Takashima-san was, that almost no one else touched her desk.

name: Yuki
message: "Putting that aside..."
The first set was, of course, Takashima-san's, but what about the others?"
Though I'd need access to a whole database of fingerprints to figure that out.
Besides...

name: Yuki
message: "I wonder if there's a better way to dust the carved letters."

name: Yuki
message: "Hmm."
For now, I'd do my best to read the rest.
It wasn't like there was no chance of it working, right?
The same word was written over and over, and each time it had been scratched out.
It was a pain to decipher.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, is this an N? No, wait, it's an M. And this is..."

name: Yuki
message: "Does this say Mamiya?"

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya... kun. I can't read the rest."

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya, as in..."
The Mamiya Takuji in my class?
Why was his name here?

name: Yuki
message: "Maybe I should ask Mamiya Takuji directly."

name: Yuki
message: "Though he's hardly ever in class. I didn't see him today either."
And he was kind of hard for me to deal with.
I didn't know why, but I got a crazy headache when I was near him.
Just because I was always on the roof wasn't a guarantee that he never came up to the roof.
Though I was always on the roof and I didn't ever run into him.

name: Yuki
message: "Oh? That's..."
The person I always ran into up here.

name: Yuki
message: "Ayana-saaan!"

name: Ayana
message: "Heeere."

name: Yuki
message: "You look like you're full of spunk today."

name: Ayana
message: "Yes, I am full of cum today."
She parried my greeting skillfully.

name: Yuki
message: "You're full of semen?"

name: Ayana
message: "No, I just followed your lead."

name: Yuki
message: "For the record, I'm not full of semen."

name: Ayana
message: "Neither am I."

name: Yuki
message: "I wasn't really talking about a physical substance to begin with, either. I meant you seem energetic."

name: Ayana
message: "I see. I thought it was one of your trademark jokes."

name: Yuki
message: "Why are you on the roof, anyway?"

name: Ayana
message: "The same reason you are."

name: Yuki
message: "The same reason?"

name: Yuki
message: "I'm looking for Mamiya-kun."

name: Ayana
message: "I'm also looking for Mamiya-kun."

name: Yuki
message: "What? Really?"

name: Ayana
message: "Sorry, I just followed your lead. I'm not really looking for anyone."

name: Yuki
message: "Ahaha..."
Her jokes were kind of hard to follow.

name: Ayana
message: "But if you're looking for Mamiya-kun..."
Ayana-san pointed down from the roof.

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? Is that..."
I saw someone in the courtyard.

name: Yuki
message: "Who's that? He's going to the old pool."

name: Ayana
message: "Now that you've spotted his shadow, you should probably chase after him."

name: Ayana
message: "A shadow is a form that you think you want to see, but it is also a form that you don't want to see."

name: Ayana
message: "So take care when looking at the shadow."

name: Yuki
message: "That's awfully profound."

name: Ayana
message: "Yeah. I sound pretty cool."

name: Yuki
message: "Nah, I wouldn't go that far."
Putting that aside...
I should probably go chase him down.

name: Yuki
message: "Um, it was around here..."

name: Yuki
message: "Ah."
Not again. Why did this happen?

name: Yuki
message: "That's..."
A human figure appeared from the base of the pool.
It turned this way.
For some reason, it looked like it was expanding.

name: Yuki
message: "What's this? I'm getting dizzy again."

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya... Takuji."
I seriously felt nauseous every time I saw him.
Why...

name: Takuji
message: "What's wrong, Minakami-san?"

name: Takuji
message: "Were you looking for me?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, I have something to ask you."

name: Takuji
message: "You don't look so good."

name: Yuki
message: "D-Don't worry about me..."

name: Takuji
message: "I'm feeling great, though."
What's going on?
He was acting a little weird.
Why was he so... confident?
I thought he was a lot more meek.
There aren't very many things that could cause a change like that.
One was mental disease, and the other was...
Drugs?
I felt worse every time we met, and his attitude was changing too.
It was possible he'd been smoking something.
But it was kind of strange that he didn't smell at all.

name: Takuji
message: "Smoking with a vaporizer, you say? Nope."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?!"

name: Takuji
message: "Wouldn't that have to be something tasteless and odorless? That would be crazy expensive."

name: Yuki
message: "What are you talking about?"

name: Takuji
message: "What's wrong? You look shocked, as if someone just read your mind."

name: Takuji
message: "Didn't you have a question for me? That's why you came."

name: Yuki
message: "Wh-What are you so cheerful for? You're a creepy guy."

name: Takuji
message: "I see. My name was on Takashima-san's desk, huh? You did a good job finding that."

name: Yuki
message: "M-Mamiya, you! Oww..."

name: Takuji
message: "A headache again? Maybe you can't bear to look at someone who's grasped the truth?"

name: Yuki
message: "What? That's ridiculous."

name: Takuji
message: "Quite. I thought maybe you would understand. I can't write you off as a complete stranger."

name: Yuki
message: "That's gross. We are complete strangers. The only thing we have in common is our class."

name: Takuji
message: "Of course. That's the relation between you and I."

name: Takuji
message: "That's why you deserve no greeting from me."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?"

name: Takuji
message: "Because I have to confirm whether or not the person in front of me is a superior human being."

name: Yuki
message: "What are you... Ouch..."

name: Takuji
message: "I'll say it again."

name: Takuji
message: "That's why you deserve no greeting from me!"
Was he always this kind of freak?
This kind of...
Ouch. My head hurt. Seriously.

name: Takuji
message: "That's right. To put it in words, you're still confined to the second dimension."

name: Yuki
message: "The second dimension?"

name: Takuji
message: "Yes! That's right. This place, right now, is the two-dimensional world. Your current existence is like that of an ant."

name: Yuki
message: "What are you talking about?"

name: Takuji
message: "How would a two-dimensional ant comprehend the third dimension?"

name: Yuki
message: "I don't know what you're saying at all! You're not making any sense."

name: Takuji
message: "One way is for the ant to observe a three-dimensional object passing through the plane of the second dimension. It would observe the object as a square flickering in place."

name: Takuji
message: "The only part of the third dimension the two-dimensional ant's imagination can conjure is its shadow."

name: Takuji
message: "The only thing those living in two dimensions are permitted to watch is the shadow changing from moment to moment."

name: Takuji
message: "Then how would we three-dimensional humans comprehend the fourth dimension? How would we comprehend the fifth? The sixth? How would we comprehend the dimensions beyond that?"

name: Takuji
message: "That's right. In the same way a two-dimensional being can only imagine us as square shadows, we must imagine the higher dimensions as shadows."
He was talking like a madman. But what he was saying...
He was referencing Edwin Abbott Abbott's Flatland. That was quite the highbrow reference.
Urgh...
My head hurt...
Why was I so dizzy?
He was...
Mamiya Takuji was staring at my face.
He wouldn't look away at all.
They say a mental patient never looks away from the doctor.
Because they can't understand how uncomfortable it is for another person to be stared at.
Mamiya Takuji never looked away from me.
It was incredibly uncomfortable.
Annoying...
I wanted to throw him to the floor and punch him until my headache went away.
Actually, that sounded like a good idea.

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya, come a bit closer..."

name: Takuji
message: "I'll be going now!"

name: Yuki
message: "Hey, I'm talking to you!"

name: Takuji
message: "I'm not too fond of getting punched."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh?!"
Why...
Could he really... read my mind?

name: Takuji
message: "Can I really read your mind?"

name: Takuji
message: "Heheh. I'll let you chase that particular answer yourself."

name: Takuji
message: "But if you can't catch it, you'll never know."

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya Takuji!"

name: Takuji
message: "Well then, I'll be taking my leave."
Mamiya Takuji disappeared.
He was so small, and he'd always been meek before.
But for some reason, I'd felt strangely nervous just looking at him.
My hand was balled up into a fist, and my palm was dripping with sweat.

name: Yuki
message: "Why's it so damn hot?"
My head hurt.
I wasn't in a good mood, either.
Was this strange feeling because of the heat?
Or something other than that?
Something felt strange.
Since that time...
The landscape I'd known had been changing, faster and faster.
The world that never changed a bit until now was slowly melting.

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yuki!"

name: Kagami
message: "You waited for us, didn't you?"

name: Yuki
message: "It's not like I care about you or anything!"

name: Kagami
message: "You can stop with that gag already."

name: Yuki
message: "But I have to follow the archetype."

name: Kagami
message: "I don't know what kind of strange world has an archetype like that."

name: Yuki
message: "You dare leverage criticism at one merely for being a bit particular with his manner of dress (picked out by his mom), smelling just a tad bit out of the norm, not conforming to the average body type, wearing a unique style of glasses, and maybe having a weird hobby or two?"

name: Kagami
message: "No. And that was really long."

name: Yuki
message: "Then what about Kr*llin?!"

name: Kagami
message: "That one was shorter, but it's still completely unrelated."

name: Tsukasa
message: "What's wrong, Yuki?"

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? What do you mean?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "You've been staring at our faces for a while."

name: Yuki
message: "H-Have I?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "When you do that, it's usually because you want to ask something."

name: Yuki
message: "W-Well, it's not a big deal."

name: Kagami
message: "What's wrong, Yuki?"

name: Yuki
message: "Nothing. Uh, have you gotten any strange emails?"

name: Kagami
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "What kind of email?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, nothing. If you haven't gotten any weird ones, it's not a problem."

name: Kagami
message: "Did something happen?"

name: Yuki
message: "No, it's not a big deal."

name: Kagami
message: "Something did happen, didn't it?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Is it about Takashima-san?"

name: Kagami
message: "Yuki."

name: Yuki
message: "It's not a big deal, but earlier, after you guys left, I was talking to Yokoyama *****teru."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Yokoyama *****teru?!"

name: Kagami
message: "Th-There's no one with that name in our school..."

name: Yuki
message: "Huh? There's not? Uh, it was the little sister of the guy in our class. I think his name was Taikan."

name: Kagami
message: "Wait, Yokoyama... You mean Yokoyama Kiyoshi?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, yeah, Kiyoshi's little sister, Yokoyama *****teru!"

name: Kagami
message: "Like I said, there's no Yokoyama *****teru. You're thinking of Yokoyama Yasuko."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, yeah, her. Yasuko-chan."

name: Tsukasa
message: "So what were you two talking about?"

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, uh... She seemed upset about something or other."

name: Tsukasa
message: "What did she say?"

name: Yuki
message: "No, it's nothing important. It's really not a big deal."

name: Tsukasa
message: "You were talking about some email earlier."

name: Yuki
message: "It seems like some weird email is going around. A prank email. Yasuko seemed really upset about it."

name: Kagami
message: "A weird email?"

name: Yuki
message: "It's nothing. It's just a little prank."

name: Tsukasa
message: "The one that says the world will end?"

name: Yuki
message: "You heard about it?"

name: Tsukasa
message: "Not directly, but I overheard Yasuko-chan talking about it."

name: Kagami
message: "What did you tell her?"

name: Yuki
message: "I told her that was impossible."

name: Tsukasa
message: "But even after you talked to her, she seemed a bit scared about the end of the world."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Even though that's impossible."

name: Tsukasa
message: "We have practice tomorrow in the morning."

name: Yuki
message: "Sure."

name: Kagami
message: "What she's saying is that we can't come get you tomorrow, so don't sleep in."

name: Yuki
message: "Oh, I see."

name: Tsukasa
message: "Do you really understand?"

name: Yuki
message: "Clearly it means I can sleep in, since the Wakatsuki sisters won't be around to bother me."

name: Tsukasa
message: "No! You need to get up!"

name: Kagami
message: "You little..."

name: Yuki
message: "Don't worry. I won't be late."

name: Tsukasa
message: "I really wonder about that."
Those two disappeared into their house.
Haa... There's been nothing but trouble.
Even I was starting to get scared.
What the hell is going on?
Huh? Who's that?
Someone was looking at me.
What? That's―

name: Yuki
message: "Ugh!"

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya..."

name: Takuji
message: "Yo, have you been studying reason?"

name: Yuki
message: "Mamiya, why are you here?"

name: Takuji
message: "You are a being of the flesh. You need to study reason, like me."

name: Takuji
message: "Your flesh is certainly strong, but you need to study reason in order to know the world."

name: Takuji
message: "You might even hit upon some consciousness that my reason has not."

name: Takuji
message: "But you can't understand one such as me unless you study reason."

name: Takuji
message: "And if you don't understand, you'll never even be able to imagine a higher consciousness."

name: Takuji
message: "The dialectical staircase."

name: Takuji
message: "Face toward higher consciousness! Ascend!"

name: Yuki
message: "Hey, wait!"
Mamiya had disappeared.

name: Yuki
message: "He's faster than me."

name: Yuki
message: "Was he always that fast?"
Was he?
For a moment, it felt as if an electric current had run through my brain. It was almost like a headache.
That feeling...
I was probably just tired.
This was really turning into a strange day.
Um...
First, I checked my email.

name: Yuki
message: "Hm... Here it is. This gross one is probably from him."
I clicked the link in Yokoyama Kiyoshi's (I remembered his name because it was in the email) message and went to the underground message board.
Hello, this is the secretariat for the Kita High message board, SAWAYAKA.
You have received an invitation to the SAWAYAKA message board from Yokoyama Kiyoshi.
Message
sup this is Kiyoshi (^_^)
here's the invitation you asked for.
go ahead and sign up.
and don't forget about the party!
a bunch of people from a certain
famous college will be coming (*^_^*)
it'll be crazy fun (^o^)/
What a creepy message.
So, to register, I just needed to click the link... This one, right?
Was it a good idea to use my real name?
Oh, but there was probably an administrator...
Eh, who cares? I could just make up a name and register. It should be fine as long as it didn't sound fake.
Maybe I should use a real person's name.
How about...
Shibata Katsuie.
My address was... Owari Province, Aichi District, Kamiyashiro Village.
Date of birth... Second year of the Daiei era.
My age was automatically calculated. 487 years old, huh?
Then a login ID and password.
Then it looked like I'd need to be approved by an administrator.
If the administrator checked for that name on the register, it might not get approved. But then I could just register with some other student's information, or I could even use my own if I wanted.
Well, it's not a big deal either way.

name: Yuki
message: "Okay, finished. Done and done."
I collapsed on top of my bed.

name: Yuki
message: "I'm really tired after all that."
Probably because I ran around so much today.

name: Yuki
message: "But still..."
People were scared.
Yokoyama's little sister...
Kagami and Tsukasa... and even me...
We were all feeling anxious.
We knew it was a load of crap, but it was still frightening.
But I suppose that's what it means to be afraid...

name: Yuki
message: "Well, I'd better get to bed."
